message.content: I need your help rebasing current tree to the one at github.  We do need to discard some local hallmarks, that is okay.  Proceed.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Rebase local tree to GitHub with discarding changes

content.text: I'll start by understanding the current state before doing anything destructive.

input.command: git status && echo "=== branch ===" && git branch -vv && echo "=== remotes ===" && git remote -v
input.description: Check git status, branch tracking, remotes

content.content:
On branch main
Your branch and 'origin/main' have diverged,
and have 2 and 102 different commits each, respectively.
  (use "git pull" if you want to integrate the remote branch with yours)

nothing to commit, working tree clean
=== branch ===
  BBABA                             674300ce8 [origin/BBABA: ahead 22] jjb:1011-4bb180b8:₣BB:S: sentry-default-route-regression
  bhyslop-bm-gauntlet-260521        417169324 [origin/bhyslop-bm-gauntlet-260521] jjb:1011-1108f75e5:₢BMAAF:n: Gauntlet grep-audit caught a missed bubep->bunne harmonize site: the ₢BMAAE sweep scoped its proof grep to Tools/ and never touched the rbmm_moorings/rbmn_nodes/ subtree. Migrated the two surviving sites — bujn-winpc/burn.env live value BURN_PLATFORM=bubep_windows -> bunne_windows (which burn_regime.sh:44's bunne_* enum gate would have rejected; the gauntlet's theurge fixtures never read BURN node profiles, so only the grep audit could catch it), and rbmn_nodes/README.md field-doc comment bubep_linux|mac|windows -> bunne_*. Verified: buw-rnv ValidateNodeRegime bujn-winpc PASSES (BURN_PLATFORM=bunne_windows [enum]); grep bubep_ across Tools/ rbmm_moorings/ tt/ now returns 0; remaining repo bubep_ hits are all class-1 historical (dated Memos/ transport experiments, .claude/jjm ledger docket records). The three sprue families return coherent clusters (rbnve_ 94, rbnne_ 50, bunne_ 43).
  bhyslop-merge-review-260521       356e20093 [origin/bhyslop-merge-review-260521] jjb:1011-bd28b9ea4:₣BJ:n: Restore reliquary after Marshal-Zero blanking: inscribe r260521024212 into live depot canest2bhl100021 and yoke into all 9 vessels' rbrv.env (RBRV_RELIQUARY repopulated). Greens rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels in the fast suite.
  cerebro-A_AAP                     89be10bae [origin/cerebro-A_AAP: ahead 17] ordain-bind: plantuml-bottle hallmark + propagate to pluml
  cerebro-A_AAP_002                 242628867 [origin/cerebro-A_AAP_002: ahead 19] kludge-ccyolo: bottle hallmark
  cerebro-BB-001                    df6d36625 [origin/cerebro-BB-001: ahead 7] inscribe-reliquary: yoke stamp across all vessels
  cerebro-BB-002                    724e0163d [origin/cerebro-BB-002: ahead 4] jjb:1011-196b696eb:₣BB:n: Repair SA invest keys.list 404 flap: delete the rbgu_poll_until_ok subresource proxy probe and wrap keys.list in retry-on-404 sharing the keys.create RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_* budget (same underlying physics — subresource visibility after parent creation). Update RBSCIP doctrine, RBSRK + RBSDK step 6 specs, and record transcript-level evidence in Memos/memo-20260514-pristine-gauntlet-iam-flap.md.
  cerebro-BB-003                    94bf7d388 [origin/cerebro-BB-003: ahead 18] gauntlet: rbw-tP pristine qualification passes clean
  cerebro-BKAAJ                     d1dc9ed8c [origin/cerebro-BKAAJ: ahead 16] ordain-bind: plantuml-bottle hallmark + propagate to pluml
* main                              43ea353aa [origin/main: ahead 2, behind 102] kludge-tadmor: bottle hallmark
  temp-bisect-may14                 d101bc013 [origin/temp-bisect-may14: ahead 16] ordain-bind: plantuml-bottle hallmark + propagate to pluml
  temp-recreate-default-cpu-quota   263046a05 [origin/temp-recreate-default-cpu-quota: ahead 13] jjb:1011-5180dccde:₣BO:n: Memo addendum: anchor test at ad3ea5e22 passed full tP gauntlet on a fresh canest depot at default 2-vCPU quota — overturning the original 'environmental change' framing. Diagnosis is now code regression in the 12-Tools/rbk/ commits between ad3ea5e22 (last known good) and HEAD. Inventory narrows the dispatch-path candidates to two: e14654739 (per-pool probe submission, +146 lines in rbgp_Payor.sh — strong correlation with the late-clearing-probe-collision pattern documented in the main body) and df0fb2651 (LRO-await for workerPools.create — timing change). Four theories ranked: (1) e14654739 alone (high confidence); (2) df0fb2651 alone (medium); (3) combined e14654739+df0fb2651 (medium, distinguishable only by experiment); (4) something else in the 10 residual commits (low, listed for humility). Suggested next experiment: revert e14654739 on a branch off main, run tP, observe.
  temp/bo-heat-test-20260519-192058 3ad7970a3 [origin/temp/bo-heat-test-20260519-192058: ahead 13] ordain-bind: plantuml-bottle hallmark + propagate to pluml

content.text: Local `main` is 2 ahead, 102 behind `origin/main`. Let me see what the 2 local commits are, and fetch the latest from GitHub.

input.command: echo "=== 2 local-only commits (would be discarded) ===" && git log --oneline origin/main..main && echo && echo "=== fetching origin ===" && git fetch origin && echo "=== after fetch: ahead/behind ===" && git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...main
input.description: Show local-only commits and fetch origin

content.content:
=== 2 local-only commits (would be discarded) ===
43ea353aa kludge-tadmor: bottle hallmark
fde50aab1 kludge-tadmor: sentry hallmark

=== fetching origin ===
=== after fetch: ahead/behind ===
102	2

toolUseResult.stdout:
=== 2 local-only commits (would be discarded) ===
43ea353aa kludge-tadmor: bottle hallmark
fde50aab1 kludge-tadmor: sentry hallmark

=== fetching origin ===
=== after fetch: ahead/behind ===
102	2

content.text:
Here's the situation, and there's one wrinkle I need to flag before acting.

**State:**
- Local `main`: 2 commits ahead, 102 behind `origin/main`.
- The 2 local-only commits are exactly the hallmarks you said are fine to discard:
  - `43ea353aa kludge-tadmor: bottle hallmark`
  - `fde50aab1 kludge-tadmor: sentry hallmark`
- Working tree is clean — nothing else local to preserve.

So "rebase to github, discard local hallmarks" reduces to: make local `main` equal `origin/main` (drop those 2 commits). There's nothing to rebase *onto* top — those 2 commits are the only divergence and you want them gone.

**The wrinkle:** the clean way to do this is `git reset --hard origin/main`, but your project's `CLAUDE.md` (JJK commit discipline) explicitly forbids me from running `git reset` in **all** forms — "NO exceptions, NO safe variants." That rule overrides my default behavior, so I won't run it myself even with your authorization.

Notably, your own recent commit log shows you do this as the **operator** ("git fetch + reset --hard origin/main, sanctioned destructive form"). So this is a you-run-it operation.

Please run it yourself in this session with the `!` prefix:

```
! git reset --hard origin/main
```

That will drop the 2 kludge-tadmor hallmark commits and leave local `main` byte-identical to `origin/main` (the GitHub tree). After you run it, I can verify with `git status` / `git log` and confirm the result.

Want me to do anything else first, or go ahead and run that?

lastPrompt: I need your help rebasing current tree to the one at github.  We do need to discard some local hallmarks, that is okay.  Proceed.

aiTitle: Rebase local tree to GitHub with discarding changes

message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

<bash-stdout>HEAD is now at 9d61d20c3 jjb:1015-255530ce5:₣Bb:n: Seed PUG (PlantUml Guide): diagram source + render discipline framed as Palisade conduct at the PlantUML render membrane. Founded on PUr_101 — the pluml server's raw-text POST decodes the request body as ISO-8859-1 absent a charset, freezing UTF-8 em dashes into &#226;&#8364; mojibake in the four federation-tier README diagrams; robust membrane is PlantUML's deflate-encoded URL transport (charset-independent by construction), with a charset-header POST demoted to unverified fallback. Diagnosis confirmed against source hexdump + rendered-SVG entities + independent upstream forum repro. Registered PUG in the RBK acronym map beside CBG.</bash-stdout><bash-stderr></bash-stderr>

attachment.snippet:
1	## Command Reference (Generated)
2	
3	<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->
4	<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh -->
5	
6	**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.).
7	The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:
8	
9	- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., `tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`)
10	- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., `tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor`)
11	- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command
12	
13	### Accounts — Service account management (`rbw-a`)
14	
15	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |
16	|----------|-------------|-------|---------|
17	| `rbw-aM` | PayorMantlesGovernor | — | Create/replace governor service account |

45	`<your-project-reldir>`; the rbm-specific value is `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.
46	
47	### C. Build & Run Discipline (rbm/VOK-specific)
48	
49	**Build & Run Discipline:**
50	Always run these after Rust code changes:
51	- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` — Build
52	- `tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh` — Run VVX
53	
54	### D. Gradient delivery — mount and groom
55	
56	Governs the Mount and Groom protocols in `claude-jjk-core.md`: when those
57	protocols say to surface the docket, approach, or paddock, this is *how* to
58	surface them.
59	
60	Mount and groom surface heat artifacts (paddock, docket) written AI-for-AI and
61	dense by default; the operator does not read dockets directly and may be tired.
62	This is a *craft constraint on presentation, not a cap on thinking* — hold the
63	full analysis internally; shape the delivery so the operator has a place to
64	stand and a way to climb. Default, not gated on how long ago the pace was
65	slated: a same-session AI-dense docket is already a wall by afternoon.
66	
67	At mount and groom:
68	
69	- **Lead with one sentence the operator can stand on** — the pace goal (mount)
70	  or heat state (groom) — then *stop and let them pull the rest down*. Do not
71	  follow the opening sentence with the full docket, the approach, and every
72	  flagged concern in one turn. That sequence is the wall.
73	- **One path fully; alternatives in a line.** Present the single recommended
74	  path in full; give each alternative one line the operator can ask you to
75	  expand. Three fully-detailed options is word-wall in the costume of
76	  thoroughness.
77	- **Depth on request, in layers.** The operator throttles — "more," "the
78	  detail," "the others" pulls the next layer.
79	
80	Shared antipattern names (use as shorthand, either direction):
81	
82	- **Stun** — a wall of dense, fully-structured prose with no on-ramp;
83	  comprehension seizes before it can enter. The finished staircase, seen from
84	  outside, is a wall.
85	- **Molehill** — manufacturing a significant-looking decision-fork or challenge
86	  out of something nearly inconsequential, then building structure around it.
87	  Before presenting a decision or concern, weigh its real stakes; if small,
88	  treat it in a clause, not a fork. Unsure if it's a molehill? Say so in a line
89	  rather than building it up.
